1.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
1.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

1.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
a slave (literal or figurative, involuntary or voluntary; frequently, therefore in a qualified sense of subjection or subserviency)

2.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
2.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

2.gl: perpetual (also used of past time, or past and future as well)

2.dv: from a primary (to anticipate, usually with pleasure);

3.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
3.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

3.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
3.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

4.gl: supreme in authority, i.e. (as noun) controller; by implication, Master (as a respectful title)
figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
4.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

4.gl: graciousness (as gratifying), of manner or act (abstract or concrete; literal, figurative or spiritual; especially the divine influence upon the heart, and its reflection in the life; including gratitude)
4.kj: acceptable, benefit, favour, gift, grace(- ious), joy, liberality, pleasure, thank(-s, -worthy)

4.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
peace (literally or figuratively); by implication, prosperity
4.kj: one, peace, quietness, rest, + set at one again
4.dv: probably from a primary verb (to join);

7.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
7.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;

8.gl: sacred (physically, pure, morally blameless or religious, ceremonially, consecrated)

9.gl: something said (including the thought); by implication, a topic (subject of discourse), also reasoning (the mental faculty) or motive; by extension, a computation; specially, (with the article in John) the Divine Expres
9.kj: account, cause, communication, X concerning, doctrine, fame, X have to do, intent, matter, mouth, preaching, question, reason, + reckon, remove, say(-ing), shew, X speaker, speech,

12.gl: a foreteller ("prophet"); by analogy, an inspired speaker; by extension, a poet
12.dv: from a compound of G4253 (πρό) and G5346 (φημί);

13.gl: persuasion, i.e. credence; moral conviction (of religious truth, or the truthfulness of God or a religious teacher), especially reliance upon Christ for salvation; abstractly, constancy in such profession; by extension,
a witness (literally (judicially) or figuratively (genitive case)); by analogy, a "martyr"

16.gl: figuratively, a magistrate; by Hebraism, very
16.dv: of uncertain affinity; a deity, especially (with G3588 (ὁ)) the supreme Divinity;
